requirements.title: Unified Status Type
requirements.description: The status module shall define a single 32-bit integer status type (JUNO_STATUS_T) for error propagation.
requirements.rationale: A single, fixed-width status type ensures consistent error propagation across all modules and is portable to any target with stdint.h support.

requirements.title: Canonical Status Codes
requirements.description: The status module shall define distinct status codes for each error condition category (success, null pointer, memory, type, size, table full, does-not-exist, I/O, CRC, reference, data, timeout, out-of-bounds).
requirements.rationale: Distinct status codes for each error category enable precise diagnosis of failure causes, which is critical on embedded targets where printf-style debugging may be unavailable.

requirements.title: Failure Handler Callback Type
requirements.description: The status module shall define an optional failure handler callback type that receives a status code, message, and user data.
requirements.rationale: An optional callback type allows integrators to hook diagnostic logging, telemetry, or breakpoint triggers into the error path without modifying library code.

requirements.title: Failure Handler Non-Interference
requirements.description: The failure handler shall not alter control flow when invoked.
requirements.rationale: Failure handlers must be diagnostic-only to preserve deterministic control flow; allowing handlers to alter execution would introduce unpredictable branching that undermines safety and testability.
